h1a: Is your edge a measured fact, or
lede: Fifteen blunt questions. They won't tell you what to buy — they'll tell you whether you're trading a process or gambling with extra steps. Your result is on this page. No email to see it.
meta: No signup to see your result

questions.q: Before you open a trade, do you write down a hypothesis you could later prove wrong?
o: One sentence — with what would make it wrong
No — I just take the setup

questions.q: After you close a trade, do you mark whether your original hypothesis held or broke?
o: I only look at whether I made money

questions.q: Do you size trades against a defined risk and track results in R (risk multiples)?
o: Yes — defined risk, tracked in R
I know my risk but don't track R

questions.q: A trade made money, but your reason for entering was wrong. You call it:
o: A lucky win — the process failed

questions.q: A trade lost money, but your process was sound. You call it:
o: A good decision with a bad outcome

questions.q: Do you review winning trades as hard as losing ones?
o: Yes — wins hide as many mistakes as losses

questions.q: Can you name your single most profitable setup — with a number behind it?
Can you name the setup that quietly bleeds your account?
When you enter, is your risk defined before the trade?
How often do you add to a losing position with no new hypothesis?
How often do you take a trade with no real thesis — bored, or the group chat is loud?
Do you exit before your target because you're afraid to give back profit?
If I asked for your win rate AND your average win-to-loss in R, could you tell me?

o: Structured — hypothesis, R, and a review per trade

questions.q: Honestly: is your edge a measured fact, or a feeling?

buckets.title: Right now, you're gambling — not trading.
buckets.body:
No written hypothesis, no records, no way to tell a good decision from a good outcome. That's not an edge; it's a slot machine you operate manually.

The good news is blunt: this is the most fixable state there is. You have nowhere to go but up, and the first move is free — write one falsifiable sentence before every trade, and mark whether it held.
buckets.tilt: where you sit: no edge, no measurement

buckets.band: You can't tell luck from skill
buckets.title: You have instincts. You have no proof.
buckets.body:
There's a process in there somewhere — but without measurement you cannot separate a real edge from a lucky streak. A green month feels like skill; a red one feels like the market's fault. Both are guesses.

Until you measure it, you're flying blind in good weather. Start tracking every trade in R and marking hypothesis held / broke. The fog clears fast once the numbers exist.
buckets.tilt: where you sit: maybe edge — unmeasured

buckets.band: You have an edge, and you're leaking it
buckets.title: The edge is real. The discipline isn't there yet.
buckets.body:
You know what works and you mostly do the work. What's taxing you is execution — early exits, the occasional no-thesis trade, adding to a loser. The leak is small per trade and brutal over a year.

You don't need a new strategy. You need to stop paying the execution tax. Measure edge and execution as two separate axes, and the leak becomes obvious — and pluggable.
buckets.tilt: where you sit: edge present · execution leaking

buckets.title: You're doing the work most traders won't.
buckets.body:
Hypothesis, R, review on wins and losses alike — you treat trading as a process you can measure, not a feeling you defend. That alone puts you ahead of most.

From here it's pruning and scaling: retire the setups that quietly bleed, lean harder into the ones with proven expectancy, and watch the decay on the ones that used to work.
buckets.tilt: where you sit: edge present · execution holding

ctaStopD: Trading Mirror scores exactly this — which setups carry edge, where execution leaks, luck vs skill. Free, runs in your browser.
ctaDeepD: A newsletter that redlines real trade reviews — expectancy, execution leaks, luck vs skill. No tips, no hype.
subD: New reviews, plus a heads-up when the deeper tools open. One blunt email at a time — unsubscribe anytime.
boundary: No stock tips · nothing for sale · no paid mentorship · no managed accounts
disclaimer: This self-check is a personal technical-research tool, not investment advice. It scores your own process from your own answers — nothing is uploaded, and your answers never leave this page. Results are a mirror, not a forecast.
